access_model_ledger.reason: Gram matrices, inverses, projectors, and coordinate graphs are available in polynomial preprocessing.
No generator-recovery theorem is supplied from bounded random codeword samples.
Coset states do not automatically expose the classical generator matrices used by the projector attack.
Standard code-equivalence HSP instances are constructed from public generator matrices, so preprocessing is legal.

falsifiers_triggered: Trivial-hull random code equivalence reduces exactly to graph isomorphism and finite graph matching recovers the planted permutation.
Most finite random samples have small hull, activating the source-linked shortening upper bound; no asymptotic tail claim is inferred.

hull_distribution_records.interpretation: Unconditional random-code hull sample. Bounded finite hulls make the source shortening upper bound practically relevant, but do not prove an asymptotic hull-tail theorem.

equivalent_match.cost_model: O(n^3) GF(2) projector construction plus exact colored VF2. The reduction is polynomial; the bundled graph matcher has no polynomial worst-case guarantee.
equivalent_match.interpretation: Recovered a graph isomorphism and verified its coordinate permutation on the complete code row spaces.

planted_records.interpretation: The planted search witness and independent null were exactly resolved through the hull projector. This removes code-specific hardness but does not solve worst-case GI.

null_match.cost_model: O(n^3) GF(2) projector construction plus exact colored VF2. The reduction is polynomial; the bundled graph matcher has no polynomial worst-case guarantee.
null_match.interpretation: Projector graphs are nonisomorphic, which certifies code nonequivalence under the iff theorem.

projector_certificate.interpretation: The basis-independent symmetric idempotent has image equal to the code.

summary: Sampled 320 unconditional random code(s): 0.381 had trivial hull and 0.978 had hull dimension at most two. The projector/GI path exactly resolved 10/10 planted/null pair sets; it proves no polynomial GI algorithm and emits no quantum evidence.

theorem.domain: Permutation equivalence of full-rank linear codes; executable workbench specializes to binary codes.
limitations: The theorem reduces code equivalence to graph isomorphism; it does not put GI in polynomial time.
The executable graph matcher is a finite baseline with no polynomial worst-case guarantee.
The direct projector requires explicit generator matrices and a trivial hull.
For nontrivial hulls this module records the source theorem's shortening cost but does not implement all GI calls.
Random finite hull statistics are not an asymptotic bounded-hull theorem.
theorem.nontrivial_hull_upper_bound: O(h n^(omega+h+1) GI(n)) for hull dimension h via shortening.
theorem.projector_formula: Sigma_C = G^T (G G^T)^(-1) G for hull(C)={0}.
theorem.theorem_locator: Theorem 6 for trivial hulls; Proposition 9 and Theorem 10 for shortening nontrivial hulls.
theorem.trivial_hull_reduction_cost: O(n^omega) field operations plus one weighted graph-isomorphism instance.
